claim: Shumailov 2024 Nature showed models trained recursively on their own outputs suffer model collapse where tail events disappear, across LLMs VAEs and Gaussian mixture models
A 2024 critique argues the model-collapse dynamics are sensitive to the replacement assumptions and do not straightforwardly generalize
Accumulating synthetic data alongside the original real data avoids model collapse across models and modalities
Synthetic-data verification before training can preserve or improve performance
C2PA embeds cryptographically signed manifests recording who created content, what tools were used, and whether AI was involved
